doc: Declarative spec. The agent substitutes <placeholders> and EXECUTES. Performance judges the money per search term, ranked by spend — pull the whole report (high limit), aggregate per term at read time. MCP paths: ../../../../_framework/porter-mcp-calls.md

queries.big_result: persist to file as data.json {columns, rows}

context.doc: All optional. target_cpa = the account tCPA if known (overrides the median benchmark); roas_target = ecommerce break-even; min_clicks_floor pins the thin-data floor.

shape.model: <'lead_gen' | 'ecommerce', optional — overrides the value-present auto-detect; set 'lead_gen' for accounts that value their leads>

notes: COST SCALE: google_ads_cost_micros is returned ALREADY in currency units (verified live 2026-06-23 on a production account). Do NOT divide by 1e6.
cost_micros carries an automatic cost>0 filter — zero-spend terms never appear ('no row' != 'no waste').
A term arrives once per triggering keyword/match-type/campaign — aggregate the BASE counts before computing CPA/ROAS; native ratio fields are wrong at aggregate.
process: process.py data.json context.json  ->  { meta, terms[], rollup }
llm_step: write the 3-string synthesis (headline = $X of spend on terms that don't convert + the top waste term; diagnosis = where the waste/winners concentrate; action = the one move now). The classification + destinations + dollars are deterministic.
